nameMeanings.name: Warner
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and North German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements war(in) ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’ + heri, hari ‘army’. The name was introduced into England by the Normans in the form Warnier (Old French Garnier). Compare Garner and Werner .
English (of Norman origin): shortened form of Warrener (see Warren 2).

Irish (Cork): when this is not the Anglo-Norman name (see above), an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Murnáin (see Murnane ), found in medieval records as Iwarrynane, from a genitive or plural form of the name, in which m is lenited.

variants.name: Garner
meanings: English (of Norman origin): perhaps occasionally from the Old French personal name Garnier (see Garnier ), but it is exeptionally rare as a personal name in medieval England and no certain evidence has been found for its use as a surname. Compare Warner .

English: from Middle English gern(i)er, garner, gurner, Anglo-Norman French gerner ‘granary’ (Old French grenier, from Late Latin granarium, a derivative of granum ‘grain’). It was probably a metonymic occupational name for someone in charge of the stores kept in a granary.
English and Scottish: commonly shortened form of   Gardner  .

variants.name: Werner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Erwin, Heinz, Gerhard, Ernst, Helmut, Horst, Klaus, Arno, Dieter.

German, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Hungarian, Norwegian, Swedish, and Danish: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’ + heri, hari ‘army’. The surname of German origin is also found in Britain (compare Warner ) and France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare Verner .
Germanized or Americanized form of Czech, Hungarian, Swedish, Danish, Croatian, and Slovenian   Verner  , a cognate or variant of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Warner was born in 1360, in Besthorpe, Norfolk, England as the daughter of John WARNER Esq and Madre de Margaret Warner. She married Thomas Whetenhall. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Warner est née en 1360, à Besthorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre, fille de John WARNER Esq et Madre de Margaret Warner. Elle a épousé Thomas Whetenhall. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Warner wurde 1360 in Besthorpe, Norfolk, England, als Tochter von John WARNER Esq und Madre de Margaret Warner geboren. Sie hat Thomas Whetenhall geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret Warner è nata il nel 1360 nel Besthorpe, Norfolk, England come figlia di John WARNER Esq e Madre de Margaret Warner. Lei sposato Thomas Whetenhall. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.Besthorpe, Norfolk, England: イギリスノーフォルクベスソルペ
ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Warnerは1360年にイギリスノーフォルクベスソルペでJohn WARNER EsqとMadre de Margaret Warnerの娘として生まれた。Thomas Whetenhallと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Warner(은)는 1360년 영국 노르폴크 베스소에서 John WARNER Esq(와)과 Madre de Margaret Warner의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Thomas Whetenhall(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret Warner nasceu em 1360, em Besthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra filha de John WARNER Esq e Madre de Margaret Warner. Ela casou-se com Thomas Whetenhall. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Warner родилась в 1360 г. в местности бестаурп, норфолк, Англия. Её родителей звали John WARNER Esq и Madre de Margaret Warner. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Whetenhall. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret Warner nació en 1360, en Besthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra como hija de John WARNER Esq y Madre de Margaret Warner. Se casó con Thomas Whetenhall. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
